acceptance_criteria.text: The EVE SSO flow matches the documented authorization-code behavior: the app redirects users to the official authorization endpoint with `response_type=code`, `client_id`, `redirect_uri`, `scope`, and `state`; the callback validates state before exchanging the code; token validation checks the documented issuer, audience, and character-subject shape.
proof: src/web/auth-routes.ts#L72-L90 constructs the authorization redirect with response_type=code, redirect_uri, client_id, scope, and state.
src/web/auth-routes.ts#L95-L168 validates code and state, marks state as used, and exchanges the authorization code only after the checks.
src/eve/sso-auth.ts#L70-L97 verifies issuer, audience, and CHARACTER:EVE subject shape.
npm run test -- tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/capabilities.test.ts exited 0.

acceptance_criteria.text: ESI requests follow the documented transport rules: all ESI calls send a descriptive `User-Agent` and `X-Compatibility-Date`, private calls stay scoped to the linked character, and the implementation does not expose raw secrets, refresh logic, or pagination internals to model-facing code.
proof: src/eve/esi-client.ts#L202-L208 sets User-Agent and X-Compatibility-Date on every request and only adds Authorization for authenticated calls.
src/eve/esi-client.ts#L88-L117 binds private requests to the linked character and requires a valid token.
src/eve/sso.ts#L240-L287 keeps refresh logic server-side and uses encrypted storage for token material.
npm run test -- tests/unit/esi-client.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/capabilities.test.ts exited 0.

acceptance_criteria.text: ESI caching, retry, and pagination behavior matches the documented expectations: GET requests use cache revalidation, error retries are bounded for `420`, `429`, and transient `5xx`, and `X-Pages` collection fails closed instead of silently truncating data when the configured page limit would be exceeded or the page snapshot changes mid-collection.
proof: src/eve/esi-client.ts#L220-L243 revalidates cached GET responses and accepts 304.
src/eve/esi-client.ts#L301-L361 retries 420, 429, and transient 5xx with bounded attempts.
src/eve/esi-client.ts#L261-L295 fails closed on paginated snapshot drift and on configured page-limit overrun.
tests/unit/esi-client.test.ts now covers cache revalidation, 429 retry, page-limit failure, Last-Modified snapshot matching, and snapshot drift failure.
npm run test -- tests/unit/esi-client.test.ts exited 0.
docs/RELIABILITY.md#L7-L17 documents the same cache/retry/pagination behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: Private ESI access remains gated by a fresh `get_eve_capabilities` check and by granted scopes for the active Telegram user/chat before any private ESI operation is executed.
proof: src/eve/capabilities.ts#L85-L107 records fresh capability snapshots and enforces a 10-minute freshness window keyed by user/chat.
src/eve/esi-client.ts#L97-L117 rejects private ESI access when the snapshot is stale or scopes are missing.
npm run test -- tests/unit/capabilities.test.ts tests/unit/esi-client.test.ts exited 0.

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository documentation and runtime checks agree on the EVE integration behavior, so the codebase presents one consistent model for SSO, ESI transport, and private-access boundaries.
proof: docs/RELIABILITY.md#L7-L30 now matches the runtime cache/retry/pagination model implemented in src/eve/esi-client.ts.
docs/SECURITY.md#L1-L19 still matches the runtime boundary around secrets and private ESI access.
src/web/auth-routes.ts#L72-L168 and src/eve/esi-client.ts#L88-L117 present a consistent SSO and private-access boundary model.
npm run build exited 0.
npm run typecheck exited 0.
npm run lint exited 0.
npm run test -- tests/integration/auth-callback.test.ts exited 0.

commands_for_fresh_verifier: npm run test -- tests/unit/esi-client.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/capabilities.test.ts
